# EduScale

> Real-time engineering learning platform with 1v1 coding battles, distributed locking, circuit breakers, and Prometheus metrics. Built for engineering students preparing for placements.

EduScale is a side project by Shailesh Chaudhari (Software Engineer at ContextQA, Ahmedabad, India). Author portfolio: https://shaileshchaudhari.vercel.app.

## Live

- App: https://eduscale.vercel.app
- Source: https://github.com/Shailesh93602/devscale

## Why this exists

Real-time coding battles look simple — two users, one problem, sync state. The hard part is making sync correct under concurrency: when many battles run simultaneously across multiple Node processes, naive in-memory state breaks. EduScale's backend uses `@socket.io/redis-adapter` for horizontal Socket.io scaling, redlock (Redlock distributed locks) to prevent race conditions in battle-state writes, opossum for circuit-breaker protection on the code-execution sandbox, prom-client for Prometheus metrics, and Bull queues for background work (leaderboard recompute, async grading).

## Tech stack

- **Frontend**: Next.js 15 (App Router), React 19, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, Redux Toolkit
- **Backend**: Node.js + Express.js (TypeScript), Prisma ORM + PostgreSQL, Socket.io with @socket.io/redis-adapter, redlock, opossum, prom-client, Bull queues
- **Infrastructure**: Redis Cluster (adapter + locks + cache), Supabase Auth, Vercel hosting
